session_contract: Pick exactly one pending task whose dependencies are complete.
Implement only that task plus minimal prerequisite refactors required to finish it cleanly.
Add or update the task's integration test before marking the task complete.
Run the task's listed test command and any necessary regression tests.
Commit the completed task to the git repository with a descriptive non-interactive commit message.
Update `agent_tasks.json` status fields and append a concise entry to `agent_progress.md`.
Leave the repository in a clean state with the next recommended task called out explicitly.

tasks.title: Initialize the long-running agent harness
scope: Add pytest and test configuration
Create tests/integration and fixture directories
Add deterministic fake adapters for Gmail, source fetch, article fetch, and OpenAI
Create temp-config and temp-database test helpers
Keep all test runs offline by default
done_when: The repo has a repeatable offline smoke test
A fake-service based pipeline run completes without network access
Test scaffolding is ready for later tasks
tasks.notes: This is the initializer task. Do not start architectural work before this exists.

tasks.title: Refactor the monolith into modules behind interfaces with no behavior change
scope: Split main.py responsibilities into config, Gmail ingestion, source ingestion, ranking, summarization, rendering, delivery, and repository interfaces
Preserve the current CLI entry behavior
Keep runtime behavior unchanged aside from module boundaries
artifacts: new module files under the project root or a package directory
done_when: The previous single-run flow still works through a thin composition layer
The new boundaries are explicit enough for later storage and job split work
The legacy-equivalent integration test passes

tasks.title: Add persistence and migrations for the central content repository
scope: Introduce SQLite as the default repository backend
Add schema or migrations for sources, fetched stories, article snapshots, ingestion runs, delivery runs, user source selections, and optional persona metadata
Expose repository methods for upsert, dedupe, and query patterns needed by later jobs
artifacts: migration files or schema bootstrap code
done_when: Stories can be inserted idempotently
Repository queries can filter by source and recency
A repeated ingest does not duplicate data

tasks.title: Build the centralized source-fetch job
scope: Replace on-demand RSS source retrieval with a standalone fetch job
Normalize fetched stories and store them in the repository
Store article snapshots or equivalent delivery-ready text for centrally fetched stories
Record ingestion runs and partial failures
done_when: fetch-sources populates the repository without attempting newsletter delivery
The job is idempotent over repeated runs
Canned feed and article fixtures can drive the full ingest path offline

tasks.title: Switch newsletter delivery to use the central repository for non-Gmail sources
scope: Remove live direct-source fetching from the delivery path
Read centrally fetched stories and article snapshots from the repository
Preserve current Gmail live ingestion behavior for now
artifacts: ranking and summarization inputs sourced from repository data
done_when: Delivery succeeds with outbound source fetching disabled
Repository-backed stories participate in ranking, selection, and summarization
The old on-the-fly direct-source path is no longer needed by delivery

tasks.title: Add repository-backed source selection in the admin app
scope: Add source selection UI to the admin app
Persist source eligibility in the repository instead of static YAML
Apply source selection during newsletter delivery queries
done_when: Users can enable or disable centrally fetched sources in the admin app
Selections persist across runs
Disabled sources do not appear in delivered digests

tasks.title: Add canned central-fetch mode for development
scope: Add a dev or test mode that seeds the repository from canned fetched stories
Add deterministic fake ranker and summarizer implementations
Ensure local development does not require live domains or live LLM calls
done_when: A full local run can complete entirely offline
Canned data drives both ingestion-derived content and delivery
Digest output is deterministic under test

tasks.title: Add persona-aware ranking and summarization
scope: Add persona.text to config and admin editing surface
Centralize prompt construction for ranking and summarization
Make ranking and the summary's why-this-matters section persona-conditioned
done_when: Persona text is configurable
Ranking and summary prompts include persona context
Two personas produce different deterministic outputs under test

tasks.title: Ingest Gmail into the same repository for full delivery decoupling
scope: Move Gmail extraction into a standalone ingest job
Store extracted Gmail links in the repository
Allow delivery to run without live Gmail reads
artifacts: delivery path that can read Gmail candidates from storage
done_when: Delivery can run from repository data only
Gmail candidate ingestion is separated from newsletter send time
No live Gmail reads are required during delivery when this path is enabled

tasks.title: Finalize the two-job production flow and remove obsolete paths
scope: Document and wire cron-friendly fetch and deliver jobs
Add structured logging and run records
Add stale-data and partial-failure handling
Remove dead on-the-fly source-fetch code
Include the Gmail ingest job in the final documented flow
done_when: The documented production architecture matches the implemented job split
All obsolete direct-fetch delivery paths are removed
A full two-job integration test passes end to end

tasks.title: Add an on-demand web preview for the newsletter
scope: Add an admin route that renders the current repository-backed newsletter on demand
Reuse the delivery pipeline without sending email
Expose the preview from the admin UI
done_when: A user can open a web page and see the current newsletter on demand
Preview rendering uses repository-backed digest generation
The preview integration test passes

tasks.title: Add an admin story explorer for centrally fetched stories
scope: Add an admin page to browse centrally fetched stories
Include source, category, publish time, and snapshot status
Keep the first version simple and readable
done_when: Admins can view centrally fetched stories in the web UI
The page shows enough metadata to inspect repository contents
The story explorer integration test passes

tasks.title: Add a seven-day TTL cleanup for centrally fetched stories
scope: Delete centrally fetched stories older than seven days
Remove associated article snapshots safely
Expose cleanup through the scheduled job flow
done_when: Stories older than seven days are removed from the repository
Recent stories remain available for delivery and admin views
The TTL cleanup integration test passes

tasks.title: Add token-efficient paywall detection and exclude paywalled stories from the newsletter
scope: Detect likely paywalled content during ingestion or delivery without using LLM calls
Store or derive paywall status in a reusable way
Exclude paywalled stories from preview and delivered newsletters
artifacts: repository metadata for paywall status if needed
done_when: Likely paywalled stories are identified without LLM inference
Paywalled stories do not appear in the newsletter or preview
The paywall filtering integration test passes

tasks.title: Move per-article summarization into the centralized fetch pipeline
scope: Generate persona-aware per-article summaries during centralized fetch
Persist summary snapshots in the repository
Make preview and delivery reuse stored summaries instead of calling the summary LLM
artifacts: preview and delivery reuse of stored summaries
done_when: Per-article summaries are generated during centralized fetch
Preview and delivery no longer need summary-model calls when summaries are stored
The integration test passes

tasks.title: Parallelize centralized summarization during fetch
scope: Run centralized per-article summary generation concurrently
Respect configured concurrency limits
Preserve deterministic behavior in tests
done_when: Centralized summarization can run concurrently
Concurrency is bounded by config
The concurrency integration test passes

tasks.title: Adopt a battle-tested URL extraction library for robust title and article metadata parsing
scope: Integrate Trafilatura as the primary extraction library for article title, metadata, and body parsing instead of relying only on custom heuristics
Prevent generic anchors like Read More from becoming the story title by preferring extracted document metadata
Keep story explorer and digest displays readable while retaining safe fallbacks when extraction fails
artifacts: integration coverage for broken title cases and metadata fallback
done_when: Trafilatura is used as the primary URL metadata and body extractor in the ingest path
Generic CTA anchors are not used as primary story titles when better document metadata is available
Story explorer and digest displays use more reliable titles for newsletter-derived links
The title extraction integration test passes

tasks.title: Collapse repository migrations into a rebuilt baseline schema
scope: Replace incremental schema migrations with a single current-schema creation path
Recreate repository tables in one pass for this pre-production project state
Keep integration tests aligned with the new baseline schema setup
artifacts: integration coverage for repository initialization after schema reset
done_when: Repository initialization recreates the current schema without applying a long migration chain
Legacy migration steps are removed or collapsed into a single baseline schema definition
The schema bootstrap integration test passes

tasks.title: Remove the temporary 48-hour test config path and restore 24-hour testing defaults
scope: Delete the ad hoc 48-hour config artifact used for manual testing
Keep the project on the standard 24-hour testing window for faster local validation
Add a regression test that locks the default testing window back to 24 hours
done_when: The temporary 48-hour config artifact is removed from the repo
The default config continues to use a 24-hour Gmail/source window
The 24-hour config regression test passes

tasks.title: Add a lightweight LLM scoring gate so only the top 20 articles are summarized per run
scope: Introduce a cheaper LLM scoring step over fetched article candidates
Only run the expensive summary-generation model for the top 20 scored articles per ingest run
Persist any scoring metadata needed for debugging or operator visibility
artifacts: top-20 summary gating in fetch jobs
done_when: Fetched articles are scored with a lightweight LLM step before expensive summarization
At most 20 articles are summarized per ingest run
The ingest score-gating integration test passes

tasks.title: Persist only servable summarized articles in the repository
scope: Write only scored-and-summarized articles into the repository
Avoid persisting lower-ranked unsummarized candidates so the repository contains only servable content
Update ingest stats and tests to reflect summarized-only persistence
done_when: Only articles with stored LLM summaries are written into the repository
Lower-ranked unsummarized candidates are not persisted as stories or snapshots
The summarized-only persistence integration test passes

tasks.title: Switch default OpenAI models to the latest comparable low-cost tier and document pricing deltas
scope: Move the remaining legacy `gpt-4o-mini` default to the latest low-cost GPT-5 tier
Keep legacy configs working by upgrading the old default at config-load time
Document before-and-after input and output token prices for the switch
artifacts: updated config defaults for OpenAI models
done_when: The default reasoning model uses the latest comparable GPT-5 mini tier
Legacy configs pinned to the old default are upgraded automatically at load time
Before-and-after token pricing is documented in the repo and the model-default regression test passes

tasks.title: Persist the generated daily newsletter in the repository and reuse it for preview and delivery
scope: Add repository storage for generated daily newsletter artifacts, including the selected stories, rendered body, and metadata keyed by day
Write the generated newsletter into the repository when preview or delivery produces a digest for that day
Update /preview and the email delivery path to reuse the stored newsletter for the current day when it already exists instead of regenerating it
artifacts: repository schema and accessors for persisted daily newsletters
preview and delivery read-through cache behavior for generated digests
integration coverage for persisted newsletter reuse
done_when: A generated newsletter is persisted in the repository under a stable per-day key
/preview returns the stored daily newsletter when one already exists for the day
Email delivery reuses the stored daily newsletter for that day instead of regenerating it
The persisted-newsletter integration test passes

tasks.title: Improve the newsletter UX across preview and delivered output
scope: Refine newsletter information hierarchy, typography, spacing, and section treatment for both web preview and email HTML
Improve scannability of story summaries, source metadata, and why-this-matters blocks without changing the core content contract
Preserve compatibility with both local preview and email rendering
artifacts: updated digest template and rendering styles
preview UX improvements aligned with delivered email output
integration or snapshot coverage for the improved newsletter rendering
done_when: The newsletter preview and delivered HTML share the improved rendering treatment
Story summaries and metadata are more readable and scannable
The newsletter UX regression test passes

tasks.title: Add one-shot deployment instructions and server bootstrap script
scope: Document how to deploy the curator on a server with the admin app and daily cron jobs
Provide a bootstrap script that can be run once to install dependencies, write service or cron assets, and print next-step verification commands
Cover admin server startup plus scheduled fetch and delivery jobs with minimal manual follow-up
artifacts: verification instructions for admin server and daily cron execution
done_when: The repo includes concrete deployment instructions for a hosted server
A one-shot script can generate or install the admin server and cron assets
The deployment bootstrap integration test passes

tasks.title: Add Pacific-time cron support and afternoon default schedules to the deployment bootstrap
scope: Add an explicit cron timezone option to the deployment bootstrap script
Default generated cron assets to Pacific time rather than server-local UTC
Shift the default Gmail, source, and delivery schedules to late afternoon Pacific
artifacts: updated deployment docs for Pacific-time scheduling
integration coverage for generated cron timezone and schedule defaults
done_when: The generated cron file includes CRON_TZ=America/Los_Angeles by default
The default schedules are 16:15 PST for Gmail, 16:25 PST for sources, and 17:00 PST for delivery
The deployment bootstrap integration test passes

tasks.title: Revert the newsletter UX redesign to the previous rendering
scope: Restore the pre-T23 digest template and preview shell
Remove the richer UX-specific rendering changes while preserving the later daily-newsletter caching and deployment work
Keep preview and delivery behavior unchanged apart from the visual rollback
artifacts: restored pre-redesign digest template and preview shell
regression verification for preview and persisted-newsletter behavior after the rollback
done_when: The digest and preview UI match the pre-T23 rendering again
The preview and persisted-newsletter flows still pass after the visual rollback
The UX-revert regression tests pass

tasks.title: Create high-fidelity newsletter theme explorations that clear the aesthetic and rendering bar
scope: Replace the low-fidelity SVG concept pass with five higher-fidelity HTML or template-backed explorations that use email-safe typography, proper line wrapping, and intentional spacing
Demonstrate each option in both preview and email-safe constraints, including dark or light-mode compatibility guidance and mobile-safe width behavior
Capture the user's feedback about ugly fonts and broken wrapping directly in the acceptance bar so the next design pass is visibly more polished
artifacts: documented rendering constraints for font choice, wrapping, spacing, and dark-light compatibility
integration or snapshot coverage for the theme exploration artifacts
done_when: There are five concrete theme options the user can review in-repo
The mockups avoid the previous font and wrapping failures
The theme exploration regression test passes

tasks.title: Implement the chosen newsletter UX across preview and delivered email and add article timestamps
scope: Implement the selected high-fidelity theme in the shared digest rendering path
Add article timestamp display for each story using stored published metadata and degrade gracefully when timestamps are missing
Keep the template compatible across dark and light mode, mobile and desktop widths, and preview and email surfaces
artifacts: updated shared digest template and rendering logic
timestamp display in newsletter story cards
regression coverage for the selected theme on preview and email HTML
done_when: The selected newsletter theme is implemented in the real rendering path
Each story displays a timestamp when one is available
The selected-theme rendering integration test passes

tasks.title: Consolidate fetch and delivery into a single daily orchestrator and update bootstrap scheduling
scope: Add one daily entrypoint that runs Gmail ingest, source ingest, and digest delivery sequentially with clear logging and failure status
Keep the existing individual job entrypoints for debugging while switching deployment bootstrap and cron generation to the single orchestrator
Update the startup and deployment assets so a hosted server installs one daily cron job instead of three separate ones
artifacts: updated deployment bootstrap and cron generation
integration coverage for orchestrated fetch-plus-deliver execution
done_when: One daily command can fetch Gmail stories, fetch additional sources, and deliver the digest in order
Bootstrap-generated deployment assets schedule the single orchestrator job
The daily-orchestrator integration test passes

tasks.title: Add newsletter open and click telemetry foundations
scope: Add repository storage for delivered-newsletter telemetry, tracked links, open events, and click events
Add a tracked redirect path for article links and an open-tracking pixel or equivalent endpoint tied to persisted daily newsletters
Record the telemetry in a way that supports aggregate admin reporting while keeping delivery generation compatible with the cached daily newsletter path
artifacts: tracking endpoints for opens and clicks
integration coverage for telemetry event recording
done_when: Newsletter opens and tracked article clicks are recorded against persisted newsletters
Tracked links redirect correctly while recording click events
The telemetry-tracking integration test passes

tasks.title: Add an admin analytics page for aggregated newsletter open and click stats
scope: Add an admin page that shows aggregate newsletter opens, unique opens, clicks, click-through rates, and top-clicked stories
Present analytics by newsletter day and over useful trailing windows while keeping open-rate caveats visible
Keep the page lightweight and repository-backed so it works in the same admin app as preview and story explorer
artifacts: repository aggregation queries for telemetry reporting
integration coverage for rendered analytics stats
done_when: The admin app exposes aggregate newsletter telemetry stats
Open and click counts are grouped into useful operator-facing summaries
The admin-analytics integration test passes

tasks.title: Harden and document how persona influences ingest scoring, ranking, and summary framing
scope: Add explicit regression coverage that persona affects ingest-time scoring, final ranking, and the why-this-matters summary framing
Document the current persona behavior in operator-facing docs or admin help text so it is easy to reason about
Verify the current prompts are aligned rather than silently drifting across ingest and delivery stages
artifacts: persona documentation in repo or admin UI
done_when: Persona influence is explicitly covered in tests for scoring, ranking, and summary framing
The repo documents how persona changes the newsletter output
The persona-behavior integration test passes
